behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff: request builder + transport wiring. A unit test of a payload helper could miss dropped labels during integration. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text focusing on request shape, not real network/model output ('request sent...includes...labels').
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client that captures outbound payload. Invoke classification with a known taxonomy and assert the captured request contains the full label set (no omissions/reordering bugs if ordering is contractual).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value.
behaviors.justification: This is response-handoff validation across API adapter and normalization/guard logic. Mocking only a pure normalizer is too narrow if wiring forwards raw value. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is deterministic guard logic ('returns a label not in taxonomy' -> 'returns unknown'), not OS/network/LLM-quality dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API client that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., 'medical'). Run classifier end-to-end within process and assert final return is exactly 'unknown'.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text isolates a single deterministic transformation ('identical raw API responses' and 'normalization step'). Real infra, module wiring, and model behavior are explicitly excluded by fixed identical input.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (including casing/whitespace variants already embedded in fixture if relevant) and assert identical output label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live provider behavior over repeated calls and consistency of model-produced labels. A stub would hide true model variability. Workflow is rejected because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-agent/multi-step pipeline in one run.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live calls against the real AI API with fixed document+taxonomy, record raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label stability whenever provider response content is unchanged; allow divergence only when provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Needs boundary check between tokenizer/budget policy and dispatch path ('before dispatch'). Unit-testing token math alone can miss accidental send. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior is pre-dispatch enforcement, not live provider/model-quality behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small budget and use over-budget input with in-process stub client. Assert either (a) truncated payload sent within budget per policy or (b) request rejected and client not called, depending on configured policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real provider-reported usage and execution through a real AI API call. A mock usage field could always pass and hide provider-side tokenization/serialization mismatches. Workflow is disqualified: single model call measurement, not chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification for representative document(s), read provider usage metadata from actual response, and assert reported input/total tokens do not exceed configured budget threshold.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is retry orchestration across API client, backoff logic, and caller contract. In-process stub can reproduce 429/rate-limit signal and verify wait+retry path. System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require real network timing semantics, only correct reaction to the signal.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub API that first returns rate-limit error (optionally with retry-after value) then success; run classifier with controllable clock/sleeper and assert one wait occurred, retry was attempted, and caller receives success instead of immediate error.
